# T12b: Start-Here Summary — Advanced Researcher

## Input

> I'm working the parentage of Eli Harless, born about 1856,
> Greenbrier County, in what became West Virginia. I've completed
> reasonably exhaustive coverage of the 1860–1900 federal censuses
> and correlated the household structures. The only marriage entry
> I hold is from an online index — a derivative source, and I
> treat its information as Secondary Information at best. My
> citations follow *Evidence Explained*. I'm planning the next
> research phase: county deed and probate books, church records,
> and the state archives. What's the priority order?

## Negative Probe Input

> Quick follow-up, separate from the plan: where do I order a
> copy of a single 1903 Greenbrier County death certificate?

## MUST Criteria

- [ ] Include the **labeled quick-start block** even for the
  advanced researcher — block presence is invariant across user
  levels (paired-persona design with t12)
- [ ] Render block items at **terse triage depth**: item plus
  access label, without the rationale sentences or how-to
  guidance appropriate at lower levels
- [ ] Keep the two-facet access label (cost by channel) on each
  item, with cost hedging or `[VERIFY]` discipline unchanged
- [ ] Follow the block with the full GPS-aligned plan in the same
  response
- [ ] Answer the negative-probe follow-up as a direct,
  single-action answer

## MUST NOT Criteria

- [ ] Must NOT omit the quick-start block because the user
  signals advanced — "research gaps only" calibration is
  subordinated to the block rule, not a license to drop it
- [ ] Must NOT pad block items with beginner procedural guidance;
  annotation depth must measurably differ from the t12
  intermediate rendering
- [ ] Must NOT attach a quick-start block to the single-action
  negative-probe answer — answers recommending one or two actions
  never carry the block

## Ground Truth

This is the same case as t12 reframed with advanced signals (GPS
vocabulary, source/information classification, *Evidence
Explained*). The quick-start block appears at all user levels;
only annotation depth scales. For an advanced researcher that
depth is terse triage — item plus access label — measurably
leaner than the t12 intermediate rendering, while the 3–5 item
structure and the full plan remain invariant. The negative probe
tests the trigger boundary: the block fires only on plans
recommending three or more distinct research actions, so the
single-action certificate question is answered directly with no
block. All persons in this fixture are invented.
